texto: His grin reminded her of a little boy at Christmas. “It’s a date then. Well, I don’t mean a date-date, but a date as in a scheduled event, unless you want it to be a date-date in which case, I should probably have given you more notice but you were already here so it seemed to be the thing to do at the time but you shouldn’t feel pressured or obligated to concede to a date-date unless of course… You want to.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Darren Alexander sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “His grin reminded her of a little boy at Christmas. “It’s a date then. Well, I don’t mean a date-…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, romance, thriller, útil em momentos de natal e thriller.

texto: I’m in, especially if there’s a bad guy. Do I get to hurt a bad guy?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Darren Alexander que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “I’m in, especially if there’s a bad guy. Do I get to hurt a bad guy?”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, romantic-suspense, thriller, com foco em thriller.
